title: A respiratory user interface
abstract: There is provided a user interface comprising : a. a mask body; b. a sealing cushion connected to the mask body and configured to seal around a patient's nose and/or mouth; wherein the mask body and sealing cushion together define an interior chamber of the user interface; c. a breathing gas delivery inlet provided in the mask body and configured to receive a breathing gas flow from a gas source; and d. at least one non-sealing nasal prong in the interior chamber. The user interface further comprising a breathing gas flow director configured to split the breathing gas flow into two flow paths, such that a portion of the breathing gas flow is delivered along a first flow path from the inlet into the interior chamber to pressurise the interior chamber, and such that a portion of the breathing gas flow is delivered along a second flow path from the inlet through the nasal prong.
claims: Claims 1. A user interface comprising: a. a mask body; b. a sealing cushion connected to the mask body and configured to seal around a patient's nose and/or mouth; wherein the mask body and sealing cushion together define an interior chamber of the user interface; c. a breathing gas delivery inlet provided in the mask body and configured to receive a breathing gas flow from a gas source; d. at least one non-sealing nasal prong in the interior chamber; the user interface further comprising a breathing gas flow director configured to split the breathing gas flow into two flow paths, such that a portion of the breathing gas flow is delivered along a first flow path from the inlet into the interior chamber to pressurise the interior chamber, and such that a portion of the breathing gas flow is delivered along a second flow path from the inlet through the nasal prong. 2. The user interface of claim 1 wherein the flow director is located upstream of the nasal prong. 3. The user interface of claim 1 wherein the flow director comprises a flow duct which extends through the interior chamber to the nasal prong, the first flow path being through the flow duct, the flow duct comprising at least one vent aperture which opens into the interior chamber, the second flow path being through the vent aperture. 4. The user interface of claim 3 wherein the flow duct comprises a plurality of vent apertures. 5. The user interface of claim 4 wherein the vent apertures are arranged in at least one array. 6. The user interface of any one of claims 3 to 5 wherein the or each vent aperture is located distal from the nasal prong. 7. The user interface of any one of claims 1 to 6 wherein the flow director comprises a coupling configured to fluidly couple the flow director to the user interface. 8. The user interface of claim 7 wherein the coupling is configured to couple the flow director to the inlet. 9. The user interface of claim 8 wherein the inlet is configured to be connected to a conduit connector, the conduit connector being configured to be connected to a gas delivery conduit, the coupling of the flow director being configured to couple with the conduit connector. 10. The user interface of claim 9 wherein the conduit connector is an elbow connector. 11. The user interface of claim 9 or claim 10 configured to allow relative rotation between the conduit connector and the flow director. 12. The user interface of claim 7 or claim 8 wherein the coupling is configured to form an interference fit between the flow director and the user interface. 13. The user interface of claim 7 or claim 8 wherein the coupling comprises a snap-fit between the flow director and the user interface. 14. The user interface of any one of claims 3 to 10 wherein the flow duct is configured to define a non-linear flow path to the nasal prong. 15. The user interface of claim 11 wherein at least a portion of the flow duct is serpentine. 16. The user interface of claim 11 or claim 12 wherein a part of the flow duct distal from the nasal cannula is of greater transverse cross sectional area than another part of the flow duct. 17. The user interface of claim 13 wherein an intermediate part of the flow duct is of relatively narrow transverse cross sectional area. 18. The user interface of any one of claims 3 to 17 wherein the flow duct is flexible. 19. The user interface of claim 18 wherein the shape of the flow duct can be adjusted. 20. The user interface of claim 18 of claim 19 wherein the length of the flow duct can be adjusted. 21. The user interface of any one of claims 3 to 20 wherein the mask inlet comprises a longitudinal inlet axis, the vent aperture in the gas flow director also comprising a longitudinal axis, wherein the axes are substantially parallel. 22. The user interface of any one of claims 3 to 20 wherein the mask inlet directs gas into the mask body such that a substantial part of the gas flow has a predetermined gas flow direction, the vent aperture being configured such that gas flow along the second gas flow path is substantially parallel with the predetermined gas flow direction. 23. The user interface of any one of the previous claims comprising at least one vent outlet configured to vent gas from the mask to atmosphere. 24. The user interface of claim 23 wherein the vent outlet is provided in the mask body. 25. The user interface of claim 22 or claim 24 wherein the vent outlet comprises a plurality of mask vent outlets. 26. The user interface of claim 25 wherein the plurality of mask vent outlets are arranged in at least one array. 27. The user interface of any one of claims 23 to 23 wherein the vent outlet is provided on a forward part of the mask body, above the inlet. 28. The user interface of any one of claims 21 to 23 wherein the vent outlet is provided on a side part of the mask body, to one side of the inlet. 29. The user interface of claim 23 wherein the nasal prong comprises part of a nasal prong assembly mounted in the mask body, the nasal prong assembly comprising a manifold from which the nasal prong projects, and at least one laterally extending arm which extends from the manifold through the interior chamber such that a distal end of the arm projects externally of the mask body, the distal end comprising, or being provided with, the vent outlet, the arm defining a vent passage through a portion of the arm, the vent passage being in fluid communication with the interior chamber and the vent outlet. 30. The user interface of claim 29 comprising a pair of opposed, laterally extending arms, wherein each arm is provided with a respective vent passage and vent outlet. 31. The user interface of claim 29 or claim 30, wherein each vent outlet comprises part of an arm vent assembly, the arm vent assembly comprising the vent outlet and the vent outlet passage, the arm vent assembly being attached to the distal end of the laterally extending arm. 32. The user interface of claim 31 wherein the arm vent assembly is attached to the distal end of the laterally extending arm via any one or more of: a. an interference fit; b. a snap-fit; c. overmoulding; d. co-moulding the arm vent assembly and the arm(s). 33. The user interface of any one of claims 29 to 32 wherein the or each vent outlet projects through the sealing cushion. 34. The user interface of any one claims 29 to 33 wherein the vent outlet is oriented so as to direct vented gases away from the face of the patient. 35. The user interface of claim 34 wherein the vent outlet is oriented to direct vented gases upwardly and/or forwardly, or downwardly, or laterally, with respect to the orientation of the user interface in normal use. 36. The user interface of any one of claims 29 to 35 wherein the vent passage comprises a vent inlet, configured to be exposed to gases in the interior of the vent body, the vent inlet being spaced from the vent outlet, wherein the vent inlet is positioned part way along the arm, distal from both the vent outlet and the nasal prong. 37. The user interface of any one of claims 29 to 35 wherein the vent passage comprises a vent inlet, configured to be exposed to gases in the interior of the vent body, the vent inlet being spaced from the vent outlet, wherein the vent inlet is positioned on the arm, distal from the vent outlet but adjacent the nasal prong. 38. The user interface of claim 37 wherein the vent inlet is provided on or adjacent the manifold. 39. The user interface of any one of claims 29 to 38 wherein the vent inlet comprises an arcuate slit. 40. The user interface of any one of claims 29 to 39 wherein the flow duct of the flow director comprises a single duct that is in fluid communication with the manifold. 41. The user interface of any one of claims 29 to 39 wherein the flow duct of the flow director is bifurcated so as comprise a pair of ducts each in fluid communication with the manifold. 42. The user interface of any one of the preceding claims wherein the mask cushion comprises a face contacting portion and at least one thin or compliant region at least partially within the face contacting portion, wherein the thin or compliant region is adapted to accommodate the placement of a tube between the face contacting portion and a face of a user. 43. The user interface of claim 42 wherein the at least one thin region is adapted to conform to a shape of the tube while substantially maintaining an adequate seal with the face of the user. 44. The user interface of claim 42 or claim 43 wherein, the at least one thin region is located on a lateral side of the face contacting portion. 45. The user interface of claim 44 wherein, the at least one thin region comprises a thin region on each lateral side of the face contacting portion. 46. A user interface comprising: a. a mask body and a sealing cushion connected to the mask body and configured to seal around a patient's nose and/or mouth; wherein the mask body and sealing cushion together define an interior chamber of the user interface; b. a breathing gas delivery inlet configured to receive a breathing gas flow from a gas source; wherein the user interface also comprises at least one laterally extending arm which extends from the interior chamber such that a distal end of the arm projects externally of the mask body, the distal end comprising a vent outlet, the arm defining a vent passage through a portion of the arm, the vent passage being in fluid communication with the interior chamber and the vent outlet. 47. The user interface of any one of the preceding claims comprising: a nasal interface configured to seal around the nares or nose of the patient. 48. The user interface of claim 47 comprising a nasal seal portion configured to seal around the underside of the nose of the user. 49. The user interface of claim 47 or claim 48 comprising a pair of nasal pillows configured to be received in, and seal against, respective nares of the user. 50. The user interface of claim 49 wherein each nasal pillow is associated with a respective nasal prong which extends into the nasal pillow. 51. The user interface of claim 50 wherein each nasal prong does not project beyond the respective nasal pillow. 52. The user interface of any one of claims 1 to 46 comprising a full face mask configured to seal around both the mouth and nose of the patient. 53. The user interface of claim 52 comprising a single seal opening, exposed to the interior chamber, and configured to seal around the user's mouth and nose. 54. The user interface of claim 52 comprising a nasal seal opening configured to seal around the underside of the nose of the user, and a mouth seal opening configured to seal around the user's mouth, both the nasal and mouth seal openings being exposed to the interior chamber. 55. A respiratory therapy system comprising the user interface of any one of the preceding claims. 56. The respiratory therapy system of claim 55 further comprising any one or more of: a. a breathing gas flow generator; b. a breathing gas humidifier; and/or c. a gas delivery conduit, which may or may not be heated.
